description: Fix bugs and missing guards in the plugin-tools build command and CLI packages discovered during backend audit.

userStories.title: Add timeout to notifyServer fetch in plugin-tools build
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the build command's server notification to have a timeout so that my build does not hang indefinitely if the MCP server is unresponsive.
acceptanceCriteria: The fetch call in notifyServer (build.ts) includes AbortSignal.timeout(5_000) matching the pattern used in platform/cli/src/notify-server.ts
If the timeout fires, the build completes successfully with a silent failure (same as the existing catch block behavior)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts

Bug: Line 203: `const res = await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` } });`

This fetch has no timeout. If the server is accepting TCP connections but not responding (e.g., stuck in a long reload), this fetch hangs forever, blocking the entire build process. In watch mode, this means the developer's terminal is frozen until they Ctrl+C.

Compare with the equivalent function in platform/cli/src/notify-server.ts line 40 which correctly uses `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5_000)`.

Fix: Add `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5_000)` to the fetch options:
```
const res = await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` },
  signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5_000),
});
```

userStories.title: Fix onNavigate monkey-patch chain breaking on out-of-order teardown
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the navigation hook teardown to be safe when multiple plugins define onNavigate on the same tab so that tearing down one plugin does not break other plugins' navigation hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: The generated IIFE wrapper for onNavigate uses a safe restoration pattern that does not corrupt other plugins' monkey-patches on teardown

If history.pushState or history.replaceState was changed by another plugin after the current plugin's patch, the teardown does NOT restore the original (pre-patch) function, which would break the other plugin's hook
Instead, teardown only restores if the current function reference still matches the plugin's own wrapper
